items.verse: وَإِن تَدْعُوهُمْ إِلَى ٱلْهُدَىٰ لَا يَسْمَعُوا۟ ۖ وَتَرَىٰهُمْ يَنظُرُونَ إِلَيْكَ وَهُمْ لَا يُبْصِرُونَ
items.verseEn: And if you invite them to guidance, they do not hear; and you see them looking at you while they do not see.

items.verse: وَمِنَ ٱلنَّاسِ مَن يَتَّخِذُ مِن دُونِ ٱللَّهِ أَندَادًۭا يُحِبُّونَهُمْ كَحُبِّ ٱللَّهِ ۖ وَٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوٓا۟ أَشَدُّ حُبًّۭا لِّلَّهِ ۗ وَلَوْ يَرَى ٱلَّذِينَ ظَلَمُوٓا۟ إِذْ يَرَوْنَ ٱلْعَذَابَ أَنَّ ٱلْقُوَّةَ لِلَّهِ جَمِيعًۭا وَأَنَّ ٱللَّهَ شَدِيدُ ٱلْعَذَابِ
items.verseEn: And [yet], among the people are those who take other than Allāh as equals [to Him]. They love them as they [should] love Allāh. But those who believe are stronger in love for Allāh. And if only they who have wronged would consider [that] when they see the punishment, [they will be certain] that all power belongs to Allāh and that Allāh is severe in punishment.

items.verse: أَلَآ إِنَّهُمْ هُمُ ٱلْمُفْسِدُونَ وَلَـٰكِن لَّا يَشْعُرُونَ
items.verseEn: Unquestionably, it is they who are the corrupters, but they perceive [it] not.

items.verse: وَمَثَلُ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ كَمَثَلِ ٱلَّذِى يَنْعِقُ بِمَا لَا يَسْمَعُ إِلَّا دُعَآءًۭ وَنِدَآءًۭ ۚ صُمٌّۢ بُكْمٌ عُمْىٌۭ فَهُمْ لَا يَعْقِلُونَ
items.verseEn: The example of those who disbelieve is like that of one who shouts at what hears nothing but calls and cries [i.e., cattle or sheep] - deaf, dumb and blind, so they do not understand.

categories.name: كَلِمَاتٌ لِلْعِقَابِ وَالسَّخَطِ الْإِلَٰهِيِّ
categories.nameEn: Words for divine punishment and displeasure

items.verseEn: Repelled; and for them is a constant punishment,

items.verse: أَفَمَنِ ٱتَّبَعَ رِضْوَٰنَ ٱللَّهِ كَمَنۢ بَآءَ بِسَخَطٍۢ مِّنَ ٱللَّهِ وَمَأْوَىٰهُ جَهَنَّمُ ۚ وَبِئْسَ ٱلْمَصِيرُ
items.verseEn: So is one who pursues the pleasure of Allāh like one who brings upon himself the anger of Allāh and whose refuge is Hell? And wretched is the destination.

items.verse: وَلَقَدْ أَرْسَلْنَآ إِلَىٰٓ أُمَمٍۢ مِّن قَبْلِكَ فَأَخَذْنَـٰهُم بِٱلْبَأْسَآءِ وَٱلضَّرَّآءِ لَعَلَّهُمْ يَتَضَرَّعُونَ
items.verseEn: And We have already sent [messengers] to nations before you, [O Muḥammad]; then We seized them with poverty and hardship that perhaps they might humble themselves [to Us].

items.verse: فَجَعَلْنَـٰهَا نَكَـٰلًۭا لِّمَا بَيْنَ يَدَيْهَا وَمَا خَلْفَهَا وَمَوْعِظَةًۭ لِّلْمُتَّقِينَ
items.verseEn: And We made it a deterrent punishment for those who were present and those who succeeded [them] and a lesson for those who fear Allāh.

categories.name: أَوْصَافٌ لِلْمَلَائِكَةِ مَبْنِيَّةٌ عَلَىٰ أَعْمَالِهِمْ
categories.nameEn: Descriptions of angels based on their actions

items.verseEn: By those [angels] who extract with violence
And [by] those who remove with ease
And [by] those who glide [as if] swimming
By those [angels] lined up in rows

categories.name: أَسْمَاءُ مَفْعُولِينَ عَلَى وَزْنِ 'مَفْعُول' تَصِفُ نَعِيمَ الصَّالِحِينَ
categories.nameEn: Passive participles on the pattern 'maf'ūl' describing rewards of the righteous

items.verseEn: It is [their destination recorded in] a register inscribed

harf.hint: The celestial lamp that marks the day.
harf.arabicVerse: وَجَعَلَ ٱلْقَمَرَ فِيهِنَّ نُورًۭا وَجَعَلَ ٱلشَّمْسَ سِرَاجًۭا
harf.verse: 71:16 — And made the moon therein a [reflected] light and made the sun a burning lamp?

deduction.title: The Echoes from the Well
deduction.intro: A community was sent a messenger, a voice of truth amidst their heedless ways. Yet, their hearts were sealed, and their actions led them to a fate tied to a central feature of their land. Listen to the tale of a people who chose denial over guidance, and whose story serves as a stark reminder for all who follow.
clues: We were a community, thriving in our land, yet we turned away from the signs of the Divine.
A prophet was sent to us, calling us to worship the One True God, but we dismissed the message.
Our people were known for our connection to a particular source of water, a central point in our lives.
In our arrogance, we rejected our messenger and conspired against this servant of Allah.
Our name is mentioned alongside the people of 'Ad and Thamud as a lesson for those who disbelieve.
We ultimately seized our prophet and, according to many traditions, cast this soul into a well, sealing our own doom.

trial.label: What Was Our Primary Sin?
options: Giving short measure and weight in trade

outcome.label: What Was Our Fate?
options: Guided to build an ark to safety

deduction.arabic: وَعَادًۭا وَثَمُودَا۟ وَأَصْحَـٰبَ ٱلرَّسِّ وَقُرُونًۢا بَيْنَ ذَٰلِكَ كَثِيرًۭا
deduction.verse: And [We destroyed] ʿAad and Thamūd and the companions of the well and many generations between them. (25:38)

translations: And not you take revenge from us except
(of) our Lord when they came to us
patience and cause us to die (as) Muslims
scramble.hint: A prayer for steadfastness and a righteous end, uttered by those who found faith in the face of tyranny.
scramble.arabic: وَمَا تَنقِمُ مِنَّآ إِلَّآ أَنْ ءَامَنَّا بِـَٔايَـٰتِ رَبِّنَا لَمَّا جَآءَتْنَا ۚ رَبَّنَآ أَفْرِغْ عَلَيْنَا صَبْرًۭا وَتَوَفَّنَا مُسْلِمِينَ
scramble.verseEn: And you do not resent us except because we believed in the signs of our Lord when they came to us. Our Lord, pour upon us patience and let us die as Muslims [in submission to You]."

verse.arabic_text: وَمَن يَتَوَكَّلْ عَلَى ٱللَّهِ فَهُوَ حَسْبُهُۥٓ ۚ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ بَـٰلِغُ أَمْرِهِۦ ۚ قَدْ جَعَلَ ٱللَّهُ لِكُلِّ شَىْءٍۢ قَدْرًۭا
verse.translation: And whoever relies upon Allah - then He is sufficient for him. Indeed, Allah will accomplish His purpose. Allah has already set for everything a [decreed] extent.

theme_question.correct: The sufficiency of Allah for those who place their full trust in Him.
options: The sufficiency of Allah for those who place their full trust in Him.
The specific timings for the five daily prayers.
The detailed laws of inheritance for relatives.

surahs.name: Al-Mumtahanah
surahs.name_en: She that is to be examined

educational_notes.verse_context: This verse appears in Surah At-Talaq, which primarily deals with the laws of divorce. Amidst these legal rulings, Allah inserts powerful reminders of His divine oversight, mercy, and provision. This specific clause comes after instructions on observing the waiting period ('iddah) with God-consciousness (taqwa), promising that for those who fear Allah, He will provide a way out of difficulties and sustain them from sources they could never imagine.
educational_notes.theme_explanation: The central theme is Tawakkul (تَوَكُّل), the profound Islamic concept of complete reliance and trust in Allah. It teaches that after a person has striven and done their part, they must entrust the outcome to God. The verse promises that this trust is never misplaced; Allah is 'Hasb'—Sufficient, the ultimate Provider, and the One who will suffice all the needs of the believer who depends on Him. It is a powerful antidote to anxiety and despair.
educational_notes.surah_overview: Surah At-Talaq (The Divorce), a Medinan surah, is named after its primary subject matter. While it lays down clear legal guidelines for divorce, its overarching message is about maintaining piety (taqwa) and awareness of Allah even during times of marital discord and separation. The surah repeatedly emphasizes that adhering to Allah's limits brings about ease and unforeseen blessings, reinforcing the idea that Islamic law is designed for the well-being of the community and individuals.
